```ruby
class FenwickTree
  def initialize(size)
    @size = size
    @tree = Array.new(@size + 1, 0)
  end

  def update(index, delta)
    index += 1 # convert to 1-based
    while index <= @size
      @tree[index] += delta
      index += index & -index
    end
  end

  def query(index)
    index += 1 # convert to 1-based
    sum = 0
    while index > 0
      sum += @tree[index]
      index -= index & -index
    end
    sum
  end

  def range_query(l, r)
    query(r) - query(l - 1)
  end
end

def solve(nums, queries)
  def compute_depth(x)
    if x == 1
      return 0
    end
    depth = 0
    while true
      x = x.to_s(2).count('1')
      depth += 1
      return depth if x == 1
    end
  end

  n = nums.length
  depths = Array.new(n) { |i| compute_depth(nums[i]) }

  fenwick_trees = Array.new(6) { FenwickTree.new(n) }

  depths.each_with_index do |d, i|
    fenwick_trees[d].update(i, 1)
  end

  answer = []
  queries.each do |q|
    if q[0] == 1
      l = q[1]
      r = q[2]
      k = q[3]
      if k < 0 || k > 5
        answer << 0
        next
      end
      ft = fenwick_trees[k]
      count = ft.range_query(l, r)
      answer << count
    else
      idx = q[1]
      val = q[2]
      old_val = nums[idx]
      old_d = depths[idx]
      fenwick_trees[old_d].update(idx, -1)
      nums[idx] = val
      new_d = compute_depth(val)
      depths[idx] = new_d
      fenwick_trees[new_d].update(idx, 1)
    end
  end

  answer
end
```

class FenwickTree
  def initialize(size)
    @n = size
    @tree = Array.new(@n + 1, 0)
  end

  def update(index, delta)
    # 1-based index
    index += 1 # convert to 1-based
    while index <= @n
      @tree[index] += delta
      index += index & -index
    end
  end

  def query(index)
    # sum from 1..index (1-based)
    index += 1 # convert to 1-based
    sum = 0
    while index > 0
      sum += @tree[index]
      index -= index & -index
    end
    sum
  end

  def range_query(l, r)
    # sum from l to r (0-based)
    # convert to 1-based
    return query(r) - query(l - 1)
  end
end

def solve(nums, queries)
  n = nums.length
  # Precompute depths
  depths = nums.map { |x| compute_depth(x) }
  
  # Create 6 Fenwick Trees (k from 0 to 5)
  ft = Array.new(6) { FenwickTree.new(n) }
  
  # Initialize the Fenwick Trees
  depths.each_with_index do |d, i|
    ft[d].update(i, 1)
  end
  
  # Process queries
  answer = []
  queries.each do |q|
    if q[0] == 1
      l, r, k = q[1..3]
      # query the k-th Fenwick Tree for [l, r]
      count = ft[k].range_query(l, r)
      answer << count
    else
      idx, val = q[1..2]
      old_depth = depths[idx]
      new_depth = compute_depth(val)
      # Remove old_depth from idx
      ft[old_depth].update(idx, -1)
      # Add new_depth to idx
      ft[new_depth].update(idx, 1)
      # Update the nums and depths
      nums[idx] = val
      depths[idx] = new_depth
    end
  end
  answer
end
